content:
The description in your screenshot is likely referencing Thunderbird Desktop on the PC rather than the mobile app!

The setting that changes the format of sent mails (HTML or plain text) from Thunderbird Android is found at ... Setting > Account > Sending Mail > Message Format

  • Plain Text (remove images and formatting)
  • HTML (keep images and formatting)
  • Automatic

It doesn't matter which of these is assigned, emails containing links will still be clickable by the recipient.

Can you please clarify what issue your experiencing, expectation or goal your trying to achieve. Again, are links working in standard HTML emails you recieve?

content:
OK. I uninstalled Thunderbird from my Phone. Restarted it Installed the Thunderbird APP Export accounts to Thunderbird Mobile

Scan QR code with Thunderbird on your mobile device 

   Open Thunderbird on your mobile device    Go to settings    Select Import settings    Tap Scan QR code and hold your phone over this code

Everything transferred over to my Thunderbird Phone APP When I open the Unified Inbox only the emails from my inbox appears The URL Links appear and I can open them Not sure why I had to spend all this time and confusion trying to fix this. My 12 year old fixed it in 15 minutes ! Hope no one else has to endure this pain just to get a simple problem Fixed.
